basic_info.name: Jiyue & 8MM Surf Clubs (亼楽 / 集楽 / Jile Surf Hostel & 8MM Surf Club) - two of the original co-located surf-hostel brands at Houhai Village, Sanya
basic_info.location: Houhai Village (后海村, also Tenghai Fishing Village 藤海漁村), Haitang Bay (海棠灣) area, north of Queens Bay (皇后灣), Sanya City, Hainan Province, China; both clubs front the Queens Bay surf beach. 8MM is registered at Tenghai Community West Village No. 70 (海棠灣騰海社區西村70號)
basic_info.operator: Independent surf hostels / clubs - 亼楽 (Jiyue / Jile Surf Hostel) is widely cited as the village's earliest opened and most atmospheric surf hostel; 8MM is a separate established surf-hostel brand on the same Queens Bay beachfront; both are privately owned and unaffiliated with national chains
basic_info.opening_date: Both clubs are part of Houhai's first wave of surf hostels (mid-2010s); 亼楽 is repeatedly described as 'the earliest opened surf hostel in the village'; exact founding year not publicly listed [uncertain]

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A - natural ocean surf at Queens Bay (皇后灣), Houhai
technical_specs.pool_size: Operates along the ~1.5 km Queens Bay natural surf beach in Houhai (not a wave pool)
technical_specs.wave_height: Average ~1 m year-round, with most days in the 0.5-1.5 m soft / mellow whitewater-to-shoulder range; occasional 1.5-2 m days during stronger SE/E swells - explicitly recommended as a beginner-friendly zone
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: No - natural waves; the schools choose inside whitewater vs. outside green-wave zones based on student level
technical_specs.wave_types: Natural beach break with crescent-shaped bay geometry; gentle, slow-peeling 'baby waves' rolling in from the east, very consistent and forgiving - ideal for beginners and improving intermediates
technical_specs.session_length: Standard private class is one session (~2 hours including land briefing and water time) [uncertain]
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - natural beach break works for both Goofy and Regular
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Natural beach break - rated one of China's top beginner-friendly breaks; longboard / soft-top dominant; year-round waves so suitable for first-timers any month

pricing_sessions.price_range: 亼楽: 1-on-1 private lesson ~500 RMB per session. 8MM: 1-on-4 group lesson 580 RMB per person, with discount for full 4-person bookings; equipment typically included for the lesson
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: 亼楽: 1-on-1 private 500 RMB; adult and kids' multi-day surf camps (lower per-day rate, less individualized attention). 8MM: 1-on-4 small-group 580 RMB/person (4-person discount available); private 1-on-1 available at higher rate [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Surfboard, leash and rashguard typically included in the lesson fee; standalone soft-top or longboard rental at Houhai shops generally ~80-150 RMB/day; wetsuit rarely needed (water 24-28 C most of the year) [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: 亼楽 1-on-1 private 500 RMB; 8MM 1-on-4 small-group 580 RMB/person; both clubs use experienced long-time local surfers as coaches
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf camps for adults and kids (亼楽); 4-person group package discount (8MM); long-stay surf-and-stay packages with on-site rooms common at both [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: No formally published membership / annual card; both clubs operate as surf-hostels where stay-and-surf packages effectively serve as the loyalty model; long-term volunteer (義工) program at 亼楽 grants free room and surf in exchange for hostel/cafe duties
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Both clubs accept group and team-building bookings; specific minimum size and pricing not published online [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round - Houhai / Queens Bay has consistent waves almost every day of the year thanks to the crescent bay geometry; peak surf months are roughly October to April (NE monsoon); summer is smaller and most beginner-friendly
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October to April for the most consistent rideable swells; year-round for beginner-level waves
experience_guide.crowd_level: Houhai is now branded 'China's No. 1 surf village' with 100+ surf shops and 50+ guesthouses; Queens Bay lineup is very crowded on weekends, Spring Festival, May Day and National Day; weekday early mornings and late afternoons are best to avoid the rush
experience_guide.booking_method: Direct via WeChat (official accounts of each hostel), Xiaohongshu DM, Dianping reservation, Ctrip / Trip.com hostel listing (especially for Jile Surf Hostel), or in-person walk-up; surf lessons usually bundled when booking the room
experience_guide.booking_platform: WeChat / WeChat mini-program; Xiaohongshu (小紅書); Dianping (大眾點評); Ctrip / Trip.com / 攜程 (Jile Surf Hostel listed); Tripadvisor (Jile Surf Hostel listed in English); Meituan (美團) for some packages
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, Chinese bank cards and cash; international card support is limited [uncertain]
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - both clubs are best known as zero-base beginner entry points; intermediate students also welcome
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Children's surf camps offered (亼楽); typical minimum age ~6-7 with parental waiver; no formal upper age limit beyond health/swim ability [uncertain]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4 out of 5 - 亼楽 explicitly runs kids' surf camps; gentle Queens Bay waves are well-suited to children; minor downsides are the busy beach scene and limited dedicated kids' facilities
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: SUP and beach rentals widely available in Houhai village through partner shops and adjacent operators; not a marquee offering of either club specifically [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Lesson photo and short video coverage offered informally by coaches and partner photographers; pricing varies and is not published online [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin primary; some basic English handled by younger staff at the cafe-bar; not a foreigner-focused school [uncertain]
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No publicly listed adaptive surfing program or barrier-free in-water lesson [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required at booking - natural beach break works for both Goofy and Regular; coaches assess stance during land warm-up
experience_guide.safety_notes: Both clubs are praised for patient, experienced coaching staff; mandatory land briefing and warm-up; soft-top boards and leashes for beginners; lifeguard coverage on Queens Bay; note that across Houhai overall, fewer than ~5% of coaches hold international ISA certification, so verify specific instructor credentials when booking [uncertain]
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Houhai hosts informal community surf jams and pool/beach parties (8MM is known for occasional pool parties at its bar); large WSL/ISA sanctioned events are concentrated at Riyue Bay, not Houhai [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Houhai village hosts 50+ surf guesthouses and hostels at ~300-1,200 RMB/night; clean comfortable rooms typically around 400 RMB; both 亼楽 and 8MM are themselves surf-hostels with on-site rooms
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Yes - 亼楽 (Jile Surf Hostel) operates as a surf-themed hostel with sea-view rooms, sofa lounge, video-coaching area, bar and open kitchen; 8MM operates as a surf-club hostel with restaurant and a swimming pool used for evening pool parties; many students choose to stay on-site while taking the surf course
surroundings.nearby_food: Houhai surf village offers a dense cluster of surf-cafe brunch spots, Hainanese seafood restaurants, beach BBQ stalls, hotpot and beach bars; 8MM is specifically called out as having 'better food' and a lively bar scene
surroundings.transport: Reachable by Didi / taxi from Sanya city (~45-60 min), self-drive, hostel shuttle, or Sanya bus routes (Haitang Bay route 2, 33, 23, Lingji 3) to nearby stops including Dongxi Village junction, Nantian Farm, Tengqiao Market and Nantian Depot
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~45 km / 60-75 minutes by car from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~30 minutes from Sanya city center; ~5-10 minutes from Haitang Bay high-end resort cluster (Atlantis Sanya etc.)
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Half-day surf at Queens Bay (Houhai) + Atlantis Sanya / Haitang Bay shopping & dining + Wuzhizhou Island day trip + Sanya old-town night market; multi-day surf camp at 亼楽 / 8MM popular with young travelers from mainland China

reviews_reputation.pros: 1) Located on Queens Bay - one of China's most beginner-friendly natural surf breaks with year-round waves; 2) Surf-hostel format means students can stay on-site and surf at the doorstep; 3) Both clubs use experienced long-time local surfer coaches and are praised for patience and good attitude; 4) 亼楽 has the village's longest-running brand reputation and best 'vibe'; 8MM scores highly for food, bar scene and pool parties; 5) Mid-market pricing (500-580 RMB / lesson) typical of established Houhai operators
reviews_reputation.cons: 1) Houhai is now extremely crowded - the village has 100+ surf shops and weekend / holiday lineups can be unpleasant; 2) Industry-wide ISA-certified coach coverage is below ~5%, so quality varies by individual instructor; 3) Limited English support compared to foreigner-focused schools; 4) Beach scene can be hectic and party-oriented (especially around 8MM), which may not suit families seeking a quiet stay; 5) Ad-hoc pricing for photos, private upgrades and accommodation - little published online
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Both clubs are repeatedly recommended in major Chinese travel guides (Zhihu, Xiaohongshu, Sohu, Smzdm) as among the more reputable Houhai surf hostels; 亼楽 (Jile Surf Hostel) has positive Tripadvisor reviews highlighting beachfront pool, cocktail bar and friendly atmosphere; 8MM is frequently cited for great food, lively pool parties and patient veteran coaches; common criticism is overcrowding of Houhai overall and the broader concern that Houhai 'is no longer the secluded fishing village it was'
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Zero-base beginners (especially solo travelers and young groups from mainland China); surf-and-stay travelers wanting the hostel/social scene; families with kids 6+ for kids' surf camps (亼楽); social travelers looking for evening bar / pool-party vibe (8MM)
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Independent surf hostels rather than chain brands; both are part of Houhai's first generation of surf-hostel pioneers (alongside Jile Surfing Club / Jix Surf Club founded 2014 by Wu Zhu); together they helped position Houhai / Tenghai Fishing Village as Sanya's flagship surf community and a Sanya Tourism Board promoted destination

verification.verification_sources: 1) Zhihu article 'Hainan surfing - this one article is enough' (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/617339364) - explicitly lists 亼楽 1-on-1 private 500 RMB and 8MM 1-on-4 580 RMB/person; 2) Zhihu article 'Hainan surf clubs - 10 high-popularity recommendations' (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/369288263); 3) Smzdm reposted version (post.smzdm.com/p/arqk43eq); 4) City8 Sanya local listing for 8MM 冲浪俱乐部 at Tenghai Community West Village No. 70; 5) Tripadvisor Jile Surf Hostel listing (Sanya, Hainan, hotel id g297427-d6933019); 6) Sanya Tourism Board page for Houhai Village (visitsanya.com/destination/houhai-village); 7) The Paper / 澎湃 feature 'I'm in Sanya Houhai Village - surfing, hostels and drifters'; 8) China News (chinanews) 2024 feature on Houhai 'breaking out' as a surf destination; 9) ChinaWondersGuide article on Houhai Village surfing and nightlife
